content.problemStatement:
Consider a sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, where initially, Ai=iA_i = iAi​=i for each valid iii. You may perform any number of operations on this sequence (including zero). In one operation, you should choose two valid indices iii and jjj, compute the greatest common divisor of AiA_iAi​ and AjA_jAj​ (let's denote it by ggg), and change both AiA_iAi​ and AjA_jAj​ to ggg.

You are given a sequence B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​. Is it possible to obtain this sequence, i.e. change AAA to BBB, using the given operations?
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the length of the sequence. The second line contains N space‑separated integers B1, B2, …, BN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if the sequence B can be obtained from the initial sequence A_i = i using the described operations, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: B3 = 2 does not divide index 3, so impossible. Test case 2: every B_i divides its index, so possible.
All values are divisors of their positions (5%5=0, 4%4=0, etc.).
Index 3 has B3 = 2, but 2 does not divide 3.

solution.approach: The only way to change a value is to replace it by the greatest common divisor of two current values. Since the element at position d initially equals d, we can always use it as a source of the value d. Pairing any index i with d changes A_i to gcd(i, d) = d if d divides i. Therefore an index i can be turned into any divisor of i, and all such choices are independent. Consequently the target sequence B is reachable iff every B_i divides i, i.e., i % B_i == 0 for all i.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, total O(ΣN) ≤ 10^5.
